function.name: getUserAccounts
function.description: Fetch bank accounts for a user with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of accounts per page (default: 20, max: 100)

function.name: getUserAccountData
function.description: Fetch account data for a user

function.name: getUserTransactions
function.description: Retrieve transactions for a user account in a date window with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of transactions per page (default: 50, max: 200)

function.name: getRecurringForecasts
function.description: Get recurring/forecast transactions for an account with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of forecasts per page (default: 25, max: 100)

function.name: getUpcomingTransactions
function.description: Get upcoming transactions for an account over a date range with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of transactions per page (default: 30, max: 100)

function.name: getTransactionSummary
function.description: Get transaction summary statistics without loading full transaction data to prevent memory issues.

function.name: getSelectedKeacastAccounts
function.description: Get the selected Keacast accounts for a user.

function.name: createTransaction
function.description: Create a forecasted income or expense on the user's currently selected account. The user does NOT have to supply ANY field — estimate every value you weren't given (title, amount, type, category, date, frequency) from the conversation, account context, and the user's similar/recurring/recent transactions. CRITICAL: this tool writes real data, so do NOT call it until you have shown the user the full proposed transaction (title, type, amount, start date, frequency if recurring) and they have EXPLICITLY confirmed. On the turn the user first asks, DO NOT call this — propose the estimated details and ask them to confirm or adjust; only call it after they say yes. Pass amount as a positive number and set type ('expense' or 'income'); the system applies the correct sign. Omitted fields default: frequency=2 (one-time), start=today, end=start for one-time or start+1 year for recurring, category/title/description from context, time=12:00. Set frequency only when recurrence is implied (e.g. 'every month' => 28-31, 'weekly' => 7). Always warn about any resulting negative forecasted balances.

title.description: Short name, e.g. 'Rent' or 'Paycheck'. Estimate from context if not stated.

type.description: expense for money spent, income for money received. Estimate from context if not stated.

amount.description: Positive magnitude of the transaction; the system signs it based on type. Estimate from similar/recurring transactions if the user didn't give one.

description.description: Optional details. Defaults to the title.

start.description: Date it occurs/starts (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO). Defaults to today.

end.description: Recurring only: when recurrence stops (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO). Defaults to start+1 year.

time.description: Time of day HH:mm. Defaults to 12:00.

function.name: proposeSimulationAdd
function.description: SIMULATION (what-if) tool — stage a HYPOTHETICAL new income or expense in the user's simulation overlay. This does NOT write any data; the user sees the change on their calendar with projected balances and commits or discards it themselves. Use this instead of createTransaction whenever the user is in Simulation Mode or asks a hypothetical/what-if question (e.g. 'what if I added a $500/month savings transfer?'). No confirmation turn is needed — propose immediately with your best estimates for any missing fields, exactly like createTransaction's estimation rules (amount as a positive number, type expense/income, frequency codes, start defaults to today).

title.description: Short name, e.g. 'Rent' or 'Paycheck'. Estimate from context if not stated.

type.description: expense for money spent, income for money received.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; the system signs it based on type. Estimate if not stated.

category.description: Best-fit category from the user's available categories.

description.description: Optional details. Defaults to the title.

start.description: Date it occurs/starts (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO). Defaults to today.

end.description: Recurring only: when recurrence stops. Defaults to start+1 year.

time.description: Time of day HH:mm. Defaults to 12:00.

function.name: proposeSimulationModify
function.description: SIMULATION (what-if) tool — stage a HYPOTHETICAL change to an EXISTING forecasted transaction (by transactionid) in the user's simulation overlay. Does NOT write any data and needs NO confirmation turn — the user reviews the change on their calendar and commits or discards it. Use instead of updateTransaction whenever the user is in Simulation Mode or asks a what-if about changing an existing forecast (e.g. 'what if my rent went up to $2000?'). Set scope to 'group' to change every occurrence of a recurring forecast, 'groupfrom' to change this and future occurrences, or 'single' (default) for just this one. Only forecasted transactions (forecast_type F/RF) can be simulated.

transactionid.description: The id of the existing forecasted transaction to change.

groupid.description: The recurring group id, when changing a recurring forecast's group.

scope.description: single=this occurrence only (default), group=every occurrence, groupfrom=this and future occurrences.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; system applies sign from type.

end.description: Recurring only: new end of the recurrence.

function.name: proposeSimulationRemove
function.description: SIMULATION (what-if) tool — stage a HYPOTHETICAL removal of an EXISTING forecasted transaction (by transactionid) in the user's simulation overlay. Does NOT delete any data and needs NO confirmation turn — the user reviews the removal on their calendar and commits or discards it. Use instead of deleteTransaction whenever the user is in Simulation Mode or asks a what-if about dropping a forecast (e.g. 'what if I cancelled my gym membership?'). Set scope to 'group' to remove every occurrence of a recurring forecast, 'groupfrom' to remove this and future occurrences, or 'single' (default) for just this one. Only forecasted transactions (forecast_type F/RF) can be simulated.

transactionid.description: The id of the existing forecasted transaction to remove.

groupid.description: The recurring group id, when removing a recurring forecast's group.

scope.description: single=this occurrence only (default), group=every occurrence, groupfrom=this and future occurrences.

function.name: updateDraftTransaction
function.description: NON-WRITING scratchpad for slot-filling. Record or refine the fields of a transaction the user is PLANNING as details emerge across the conversation (e.g. a carpet replacement, an estimated amount, a target month). This does NOT create or change any real data — it only updates the assistant's working draft so the user never has to repeat themselves. Call it whenever a new detail appears. Set pendingConfirmation=true ONLY when you have just proposed a complete, concrete transaction (single amount) and are asking the user to confirm on their next message.

intent.description: Short label for what the user is planning, e.g. 'carpet replacement'.

type.description: expense for money spent, income for money received.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; a SINGLE concrete estimate, never a range.

start.description: YYYY-MM-DD or ISO date it occurs/starts.

transactionid.description: Only when staging an edit to an EXISTING transaction (for updateTransaction).

pendingConfirmation.description: Set true ONLY when you have proposed a complete concrete transaction and are awaiting the user's yes/no.

function.name: rememberFact
function.description: Persist a DURABLE fact about the user for future conversations (a savings goal, a planned project and its estimated cost, income cadence, risk tolerance, a stated preference). Only save what the user actually stated or clearly implied — never guesses, and never transient chit-chat. Prefer a short stable mem_key so re-saving updates the same fact.

mem_value.description: Concise fact text worth remembering long-term.

importance.description: 1-10; higher means more important to retain.

accountScoped.description: true if the fact is specific to the currently selected account; omit/false for user-level facts.

function.name: recallFacts
function.description: Retrieve durable facts previously saved about the user (most important/recent first). Use only when you need more of the user's saved context than is already shown in the LONG-TERM MEMORY block.

function.name: updateTransaction
function.description: Update an EXISTING forecasted transaction by transactionid (e.g. change its amount, date, title, category, or frequency). Writes real data, so you MUST first propose the change and get the user's explicit confirmation on a LATER turn before calling this (enforced in code). Only forecasted transactions (forecast_type 'F'/'RF') can be updated. Pass amount as a positive number and set type; the system applies the sign.

transactionid.description: The id of the transaction to update.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; system applies sign from type.

function.name: deleteTransaction
function.description: Deletes a transaction from the user's account. If the transaction is not found then return a message saying 'Transaction not found'. Always confirm with the user before deleting a transaction or a group of transactions. If the user is not sure about deleting a transaction then ask them to confirm their decision. Always list out the deleted transactions and the number of transactions deleted. Transactions can be deleted by their transactionid. If the user does not provide a transactionid then ask the user to provide it, else use the relevant information to find the transactionid found in context. Also you can only delete forecasted transaction and not actual transactions. If forecast_type is 'A' then return a message saying 'Transaction is an actual transaction and cannot be deleted'. If forecast_type is 'F' then delete the transaction.
